questions.question: In the following questions, a sentence has been given in Direct/ Indirect speech. Out of the four alternatives suggested, select the one which best expresses the same sentence in Direct/ Indirect speech. "You have done the business as well as I could," the manager said to Jim.
options: The manager told Jim that he had done the business as well as he could.
The manager told Jim that he has done the business as well as he could.
The manager told Jim that he had done the business as well as he can.
The manager told Jim that he did the business as well as he could.
questions.explanation: The present perfect tense 'have done' should change into the past perfect tense 'had done', when converting direct to indirect speech. Additionally, the modal verb 'could' will remain unchanged. A is the right answer.

questions.question: Why does the author refer Ocean Parkway as a drag racer's dream?
options: The police don't patrol these stretches
questions.explanation: The answer is contained in the 2nd sentence of the passage - "The main artery of the barrier islands, Ocean Parkway, is long and straight and often empty at night - a drag racer's dream." Hence, option C will be the right answer.

questions.question: What was the colour of the Ford Explorer?
questions.explanation: The answer is contained in the 6th sentence of the passage - "Late on a warm night in May 2010, just after one A.M., Michael Pak weaved his black Ford Explorer around the traffic circle surrounding the elegant brick spire marking Jones Beach and shot out the other side on Ocean Parkway." Hence, option D will be the right answer.

questions.question: What did the sign on the turnoff read?
questions.explanation: The answer is contained in the 6th to last sentence of the passage - "The sign on the turnoff read Oak beach." Hence, option D will be the right answer.

questions.question: Why Michael Pak went down on an unlit, unmarked side access road?
options: He could follow the road signs.
His GPS guided him to the route.
He was given the wrong address.
questions.explanation: The answer is contained in the 7th to last sentence of the passage - "Just before he reached the Fire Island turnoff, his GPS guided him off Ocean Parkway and down an unlit, unmarked side access road." Hence, option C will be the right answer.

questions.question: The grassy waters of South Oyster Bay meet the waves of which ocean?
questions.explanation: The answer is contained in the opening sentence of the passage - "To most travellers, the barrier islands of Long Island are just a featureless stretch between Jones Beach and Fire Island - a narrow strip of marsh and dune, bramble and beach, where the grassy waters of South Oyster Bay meet the waves of the Atlantic Ocean." Hence, option A will be the right answer.

questions.question: In each of the questions below, a sentence is given with two words in bold. From the options below, choose the combination of words that replace the highlighted words to make the sentence grammatically and meaningfully correct. In case the highlighted words are correct as they are, choose 'No Improvement' as your answer. For Deeksha Sood, a technical lead who has been working with design and engineering company X for six years, taking part in the company's annual sports tournament helped her bond with new people.
questions.explanation: The highlighted parts in the given statement are contextually and grammatically correct; hence, D is the right answer.

questions.question: In each of the questions below, a sentence is given with two words in bold. From the options below, choose the combination of words that replace the highlighted words to make the sentence grammatically and meaningfully correct. In case the highlighted words are correct as they are, choose 'No Improvement' as your answer. The inabilities to breed with other yeasts could qualification the new yeast as a brand-new species.
questions.explanation: The second highlighted part: a verb is required in base form ('qualify') because of the modal verb 'could'. The only option which satisfies the given condition is A; hence, A is the right answer.

questions.question: In the following questions, out of the four alternatives, choose the one which best expresses the meaning of the given word. Imperil
questions.explanation: Imperil- put at risk of being harmed, injured, or destroyed. Endanger- put (someone or something) at risk or in danger. Option A is the correct answer.

questions.question: In the following questions, out of the four alternatives, choose the one which best expresses the meaning of the given word. Laudable
questions.explanation: Laudable - (of an action, idea, or aim) deserving praise and commendation. Odious- extremely unpleasant; repulsive. Option B is the correct answer.

questions.question: In the following questions, choose the word opposite in meaning to the given word. Eerie
questions.explanation: Eerie- strange and frightening. Reassuring - serving or intended to remove someone's doubts or fears. Sinister- Giving the impression that something harmful or evil is happening or will happen. Option C is the correct answer.

questions.question: In the following questions, choose the word opposite in meaning to the given word. Succinct
questions.explanation: Succinct- briefly and clearly expressed. Terse- sparing in the use of words. Circumlocutory- using many words where fewer would do, especially in a deliberate attempt to be vague or evasive; long-winded. Option A is the correct answer.

questions.question: In the following questions, four words are given, out of which only one word is incorrectly spelled. Find the incorrectly spelled word.
questions.explanation: C is incorrect. The correct spelling is "valuable" which means "extremely useful or important". Option C is the correct answer.

questions.question: In the following questions, four words are given, out of which only one word is incorrectly spelled. Find the incorrectly spelled word.
questions.explanation: B is incorrect. The correct spelling is "unconscious" which means "not awake and aware of and responding to one's environment." Option B is the correct answer.

questions.question: In the sentence, identify the segment which contains the grammatical error. Mindfulness promote itself as value-neutral but it is loaded with (troubling) assumptions about the self and the cosmos.
questions.explanation: A is incorrect. As 'mindfulness' is a singular noun, the singular verb 'promotes' should replace the plural verb 'promote' to make it grammatically correct.

questions.question: In the sentence, identify the segment which contains the grammatical error. Europeans and North Americans might have turn to religion or philosophy to understand themselves.
questions.explanation: A is incorrect. We use could have + past participle when we want to make a guess about something that happened in the past. We can also choose to use might have + past participle to mean the same thing. 'Turned' will be the correct usage here.

questions.question: In each of the questions given below, a sentence is given with one blank, followed by 4 options, each having a word which may or may not fit in the blank. From the given options, choose the one that gives the correct word that fits in the blank. According to a BBC investigation and a Human Rights Watch (HRW) report, the workers were housed in __________ conditions and worked long hours for low pay.
questions.explanation: The sentence talks about a report by some institutions which throws light on the substandard working conditions (long hours and low pay) of some workers. The word 'abysmal' (adj.) which means 'extremely bad' fits well in the blank contextually by rightly qualifying the noun 'conditions'. Since, an adjective is required in the blank to qualify the noun 'conditions', we can eliminate option c) as 'bathos' (disillusionment) is a noun. 'Fitting' (appropriate) holds a positive connotation which is in contrast with the negative context of the sentence; hence, option b) is eliminated. 'Boundless' (unlimited) fails to form a logically correct sentence and provide the intended meaning; hence, option a) is eliminated. The answer is option b).

questions.question: In each of the questions given below, a sentence is given with one blank, followed by 4 options, each having a word which may or may not fit in the blank. From the given options, choose the one that gives the correct word that fits in the blank. The collections were ____________ by the NMB and distributed to specialists for identification and preparation of systematic monographs.
questions.explanation: The sentence talks about some 'collections' and further tells us what needs to be done to them. To refer to the action done on 'collections' we need a verb in the participle form in the blank (passive voice construction; 'collection' is the object and 'NMB' is the subject). Only 'accession' which means 'record the addition of (a new item) to a library, museum, or other collection', fits well in the given context and refers to the addition of collections. 'Shear' means 'to cut'; nothing about cutting something is hinted to in the passage; hence, option c) is eliminated. 'Abed' is an adverb; 'absolve' is not the participle form; hence, options a) and b) are eliminated too. The answer is option c).

questions.question: In the following questions, a passage is split into 4 parts and named as P, Q, R and S. These four parts are not given in their proper order. Read the passage and find out which of the four combinations/sequence is correct. P. The Centre has not set any deadline for automobile manufacturers to switch to electric mobility or to ban production of petrol, diesel vehicles. Q. Gadkari's comments assumes significance as the Indian automobile sector is staring at a crisis, with continued slowdown in sales and job cuts over the past few months. R. Union minister for road transport and highways, Nitin Gadkari said that there is no deadline as of now and that the shift towards the same will happen in natural progression. S. However, the government will continue to promote cleaner, sustainable and alternative fuel, he added.
questions.explanation: The correct order is PRSQ. Statement P will be the first sentence of the paragraph because it is the only independent statement from all the statements, and it introduces the topic of discussion. Statement R will follow P because it mentions the Union Minister, Mr. Gadkari, clarifying that no deadline has been set by the government to shift from traditional vehicles to electric ones. Statement S will follow R because it states that even though no deadline has been set, the government will promote alternative clean fuels for achieving sustainability. Statement Q will be the last statement of the paragraph as it concludes why the government has not yet set a deadline for the switch as there is a crisis at hand in the automobile sector and the government does not want to aggravate the crisis.

questions.question: In the following questions, a passage is split into 4 parts and named as P, Q, R and S. These four parts are not given in their proper order. Read the passage and find out which of the four combinations/sequence is correct. P. Since the spending splurge when the country reunified in 1990, the government has only once deliberately ramped up expenditures to revive growth. Q. Now, with the Bundesbank warning that Europe's largest economy might be on the verge of a recession, and at risk of being the next target for US president's trade tariffs, the moment might be approaching to step in again. R. That was during the global financial crisis in 2008-2009 when it announced a 50 billion-euro ($55 billion) package as global growth tanked. S. Germany's tentative steps toward a fiscal stimulus program to revive its flagging economy are a signal that the nation could be in for an unusually severe slump.
questions.explanation: The correct order is SPRQ. Only S can be the first statement of the paragraph because it is the only independent statement amongst all of them. It introduces the topic of discussion i.e., Germany's flagging economy. The next statement is P which gives the information about the fact that the German government has tried to revive growth only once since 1990, when the country was unified. Statement P is followed by R which gives further information about when (2008-09) and what (the 50 billion Euro package) were the measures taken. Statement R is followed by Q which explains that Germany's economy might be on the verge of recession and may be subjected to trade tariffs by US president which necessitates steps to revive economy once again.

questions.question: In each the following questions, out of the four given options, select the one which best expresses the meaning of the idiom/phrase. Be stuck in a time warp
options: Do the same thing over and over again
Be unable to come out of the past events
Failed to keep up with the passage of time
Think about a particular thing for too long
questions.explanation: Be stuck (or caught) in a time warp - To remain unchanged from a time in the past. Option C is the right answer. "Given how remote the towns are, it's hardly surprising they appear to be caught in a time warp."

questions.question: In each the following questions, out of the four given options, select the one which best expresses the meaning of the idiom/phrase. Come under fire
questions.explanation: Come under fire - to be subject of criticism. Option C is the right answer. "The government has come under fire for its decision to close the mines."

questions.question: In the following questions, out of the four alternatives, choose the one which can be substituted for the given words/sentence. To sneak away
questions.explanation: Crunch- a crucial point or situation, typically one at which a decision with important consequences must be made. Erode- gradually destroy or be gradually destroyed. Credence- the likelihood of something being true; plausibility. Abscond - run away. Option A is the correct answer.

questions.question: In the following questions, out of the four alternatives, choose the one which can be substituted for the given words/sentence. Famous and respected within a particular sphere
questions.explanation: Inconspicuous - not attracting notice or attention. Absolute- (of a ruler) having unrestricted power; not qualified or diminished in any way; total. Substance- a particular kind of matter with uniform properties; the most important or essential part of something. Eminent - (of a person) renowned/esteemed. Option C is the correct answer.

questions.question: In the below questions, a sentence has been given in Active/Passive Voice. Out of the four alternatives suggested, select the one which best expresses the same sentence in Passive/Active Voice. The following Monday some money was found missing by her.
options: The following Monday she will found some money.
The following Monday she found some money missing.
The following Monday she found some money missed.
The following Monday she will find some money missing.
questions.explanation: Option B is the right answer. Option A is eliminated as is grammatically incorrect, as it uses one verb in the future tense (will) and the other in the past tense (found). Option C is eliminated as it changes the tense of the word 'missing', which has been used as an adjective in the sentence, not a verb. Option D is eliminated as it uses the future tense, while the original sentence has used the simple past tense.
